description: Priority 4 - Cap every unbounded findMany in API routes with take + pagination or explicit justification comments.
steps: 1. app/api/admin/categories/route.ts: Add comment '// Categories are admin-managed, typically <50 rows' to justify no take limit (this is a small fixed-size config table).
2. app/api/admin/staff/route.ts: Add take: 200 and support offset pagination via query params. Staff list is bounded but could grow.
3. app/api/admin/offers/route.ts: Add take: 100 with offset pagination support.
4. app/api/admin/banners/route.ts: Add comment '// Banners are admin-managed, typically <20 rows' to justify no take limit.

5. app/api/admin/export/orders/route.ts: Add a required date-range filter (from/to query params). If no date range provided, default to last 90 days. Consider streaming the CSV response instead of loading all rows into memory (use ReadableStream).
6. app/api/orders/route.ts GET handler (line ~287): Add take: 50 with cursor pagination for the order listing.

7. app/api/admin/push-broadcast/route.ts: The push subscription findMany calls are for sending notifications to all subscribers - add comment justifying this as intentional (must reach all subscribers). The settings history findMany at line 35 already has orderBy, add take: 20.
8. app/api/admin/delivery-slabs/route.ts: Add comment '// Delivery fee slabs are admin-managed, typically <10 rows' to justify no take limit.
9. app/api/orders/[id]/approval/route.ts: The pendingLogs and refreshedItems findMany are scoped to a single order (bounded). Add comments explaining they are order-scoped.
10. Verify no other findMany calls in app/api exist without take or justification.
acceptance_criteria: Every findMany in app/api has either a take clause or an explicit comment explaining why it is safe to be unbounded
Export orders endpoint requires or defaults to a date range
